question: What is one paired observation?
reference_answer: One unit measured under two or more conditions.

question: What does Friedman test?
reference_answer: Whether several paired conditions have different ranks overall.

question: Why one Friedman p-value?
reference_answer: It is one overall test across all condition columns.

question: What does Wilcoxon test?
reference_answer: Whether one paired condition difference has median zero.

question: Does Wilcoxon only count signs?
reference_answer: No. It ranks absolute differences and uses signs.

question: What does Bonferroni do?
reference_answer: It multiplies raw p-values by the number of tests.

question: When should correction be used?
reference_answer: When many related p-values are tested and reported.
